---
title: "Teaching"
---

I teach risk management and insurance courses across the undergraduate, master's, and Ph.D. programs at FSU. For current schedules, see the [FSU Department of Risk Management/Insurance](https://business.fsu.edu/departments/rmi).

## Florida State University

::: {.course-entry}
**RMI 3011 — Risk Management/Insurance**<br>
Undergraduate.<br>
Introduction to the principles of risk management and insurance and their application to personal and business pure risk problems.<br>
[[Catalog](https://m.fsu.edu/default/course_catalog/detail?area=RMI&course=RMI+3011&term=all)]
:::

::: {.course-entry}
**RMI 4115 — Life/Health Insurance**<br>
Undergraduate.<br>
Personal and business life and health insurance needs, plan characteristics, rating, underwriting, and financial analysis.<br>
[[Catalog](https://m.fsu.edu/default/course_catalog/detail?area=RMI&course=RMI+4115&term=all)]
:::

::: {.course-entry}
**RMI 4292 — Property and Casualty Insurer Operations**<br>
Undergraduate.<br>
The composition, financial structure, and operations of the property-casualty insurance industry, with special consideration of consumer problems and regulatory responses.<br>
[[Catalog](https://m.fsu.edu/default/course_catalog/detail?area=RMI&course=RMI+4292&term=all)]
:::

::: {.course-entry}
**RMI 5710 — Insurance Company Operations**<br>
Master's (online).<br>
Fundamentals of risk, the management of pure risk, insurance mechanisms, insurer operations, and the evolution of enterprise risk management.<br>
[[Catalog](https://m.fsu.edu/default/course_catalog/detail?area=RMI&course=RMI+5710&term=all)]
:::

::: {.course-entry}
**RMI 6395 — Seminar on the Theory of Risk and Insurance**<br>
Ph.D.<br>
Review of the theoretical foundations of risk and insurance: the concept of risk, contributions from related disciplines, determinants of insurance consumption and risk management decisions, and industry dynamics.<br>
[[Catalog](https://m.fsu.edu/default/course_catalog/detail?area=RMI&course=RMI+6395&term=all)]
:::

## University of Georgia

::: {.course-entry}
**RMIN 4000 — Risk Management and Insurance**<br>
Undergraduate (Summer 2014 – Spring 2017).<br>
Risk identification, risk evaluation, and risk treatment methods, with emphasis on insurance as a risk management tool. Designed for non-majors as well as a foundation for advanced RMI coursework.
:::

## Doctoral Mentoring

**Committee member, Florida State University:**

- Gabriel Carrillo (Risk Management and Insurance, 2025) — University of Central Florida.
- William D. Cather (Accounting, 2025) — Oklahoma State University.
- Dana Telljohann (Risk Management and Insurance, 2025) — University of Cincinnati.

**External committee member:**

- Yuchen Qi (Finance, University of North Texas).
- Dan Yang (Risk Management and Insurance, University of Georgia, 2022) — Troy University.
